title: fix: use black logo on authorize/BYOP screen
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Back in black
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky CSS bug that made our logo invisible on the authorization screen. White on light green is a bold design choice, but we prefer you actually seeing our name.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue on the authorize/BYOP screen where the logo was incorrectly inverted to white against a light background.
gist.impact: Users will now see the pollinations logo clearly in black on the authorization screen, improving readability and brand visibility.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee fixing a contrast issue by painting a white logo black on a lime green canvas, accompanied by the monitor robot.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, using a brush to paint a chunky pixel logo black on a bright lime green (#ecf874) canvas. A retro monitor robot stands nearby holding a palette of dark purple (#110518) paint. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
